content:
Title
* There are two spaces between "Multi-Scale" and "Converter". Please remove one of them.

Goal
* The word "From" is missing in point 6.

* Please specify the unit symbols for each scale and format them as {{constants}}.

Input
* Line 1: Please remove the example.

Output / Cases
* Please format "°C", "°F" and "°K" as {{constants}}.

* Consider removing the degree symbol (°) from output because it's a non-ASCII character. In some programming languages it may require additional handling, which could be beyond the scope of a beginner-level exercise.

Constraints
* Please remove "15 ≤ [[convert]] ≤ 30". It's unnecessary since the full list of possible values for [[convert]] is already provided.

* Please remove all quotation marks.

* Please format each of the 6 strings as a {{constant}}.

Input / Stub generator / Cases
* Line 2: Please be consistent regarding the type of [[temperature]].
  • If it should be an integer, update the stub generator accordingly.
  • If it should be a float, update the Input section and add several pairs of cases with floats as inputs.

Cases
* Please remove the trailing newlines from the outputs of the cases below:
  • Fahrenheit to Celsius (Test 3)
  • Celsius to Kelvin (Test 4)
  • Validator 4
  • Kelvin to Celsius (Test 5)
  • Kelvin to Fahrenheit (Test 7)

* Please add several pairs of cases with negative inputs.

* The first constraint states "-273 <= temperature <= 10000" yet there are no cases with temperatures above 1000. Additionally, the constraint is violated by the case "Celsius to Kelvin (Test 4)". Please revise the cases so that the full range of temperatures is uniformly covered by the cases.

* Please follow up the comment raised by @Mathias_the_Bot.

Thanks.

I have an issue, obviosuly the results can be negative, however you only use that in the validators, never in the testcases. You also have no negative input. You also only have one testcase with a fractional input.

Right! Thanks for your comment.
By the way, what was the fractional input you're saying about?
I have updated my contribution. Sorry for taking so long to fix. If you have any more objections, pls give me more suggestions.

No worries. Below is the updated list of points to follow up:

Goal
* Please change the middle paragraph to:

The supported scales are Celsius ({{C}}), Fahrenheit ({{F}}), and Kelvin ({{K}}).

* In the six points, please format "C", "F" and "K" as [[variables]] instead of {{constants}}.

Constraints
* Please change 10000 to 1000 as there are no cases with temperatures above 1000.

Cases
* Please remove the trailing newlines from the output of the cases below:
  • Fahrenheit to Celsius (Test 3)
  • Celsius to Kelvin (Test 4)
  • Validator 4
  • Kelvin to Fahrenheit (Test 7)

* Kelvin to Celsius (Test 5) violates the first constraint. Please use a POSITIVE temperature for the case.

* Please add three pairs of cases where both the input [[temperature]] and the output are negative numbers.

Thanks.
I thought you had one that was 39.9
I have updated that test case with 39.9 to 40
